name: TypeScript filter(Boolean) Does Not Narrow Types
description: Array.filter(Boolean) removes falsy values at runtime but TypeScript does not narrow the type from T|null|undefined to T. Use a type predicate function instead: .filter((x): x is T => !!x)

trigger.errorMessage: TypeScript error after .filter(Boolean) — the resulting array type still includes null/undefined

steps.description: Replace .filter(Boolean) with .filter((x): x is NonNullType => !!x) where NonNullType is the expected non-null type.
